slides.lead: Demonstration deck for the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and exists only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Place the studio name, a one line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Wrap one word in em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared idea of restraint.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses initials in a circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Principles, materials and the discipline of leaving things out.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Minimalism is not empty space. It is the result of removing everything that does not earn its place.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, a strong illustration, a short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: lower operational energy across our completed homes
saved on site through prefabricated modules
of clients return for a second commission
bullets: Measure outcome, not effort: what changes for the people who live there.
Start where the constraint is real, not where the brief is loudest.
A small built room beats a large rendered plan.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: We design by subtraction, not addition.
bullets: To the site, the light and the way you actually live.
Until only the load bearing ideas remain.
Every joint and edge resolved with care.
With makers who share the same standard.
slides.footnote: // two column slide with an illustration
slides.notes: Two column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same brief, two attitudes and two very different rooms.
cards: Surfaces carry the meaning. Rich, but quick to date.
Every wall does something. Generous, often restless.
Space and light carry the meaning. Calm and lasting.
The few right moves, repeated with discipline.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or a short list of variants.

slides.lead: Six recurring building blocks of a quiet interior.
cards: Orientation, light and view set the plan.
A clear structural grid keeps spaces honest.
Daylight as the primary material, free and alive.
A short, tactile list of honest finishes.
Every edge drawn, nothing left to chance on site.
Makers who hold the same standard we do.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: The rooms reach a calm that asks for nothing more.
Removing one wall lets light reshape the whole plan.
Restraint today leaves room to grow slowly over years.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear deliverable.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: The best room is the one you stop noticing, because everything in it is exactly where it should be.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a strong left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget on a busy project.
bullets: Each space carries a single clear intention.
Show what a thing is made of, never fake it.
Every line on the drawing must justify itself.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A room without restraint feels busy, even when it is empty.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to expect this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the model and move through the house live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or a break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
